raw_consumer_statement:
I realized that XXXX has been charging me every month since XX/XX/year> for between {$16.00} and {$21.00} for a total of {$1500.00}. They used deceptive practices, I never would have signed up for a XXXX account if I was not told by XXXX that it was free. They are owned by the same company and this is why I had the XXXX account that I barely used. When you sign up for shipstation they explicitly tell you that you have a free XXXX account due to the shared ownership. When you see the share balance between the accounts, you assume that they are connected correctly. 

I've since stopped using my XXXX account and I can still see that they have a shared balance, but apparently it was not set up correctly. They've essentially been double charging me. 

I learned at American Express about the deceptive practices by XXXX at XXXX, and they did not offer me any resolution except to refund the past 60 days, which is about {$40.00}. American Express has a duty to protect consumers from deceptive merchants that are using their cards, and they've not held up their standard.
